== Notes for [[Item:Q104603|Q104603]] ==

Regidos de la ciudad de Oviedo?

Juan del Castillo must have come from a influential family in order to marry onto the Diaz de Cuellar family. His wife, Maria Diaz de Cuellar's family was at the top of Spanish society in Mexico. Her mothers father was Gonzalo Salazar, the lieutenant governor of all New Spain and Factor of the Royal Treasury in Mexico. Her mother's second husband was Christobal de Narriando Ruy de Ornate and this marriage gave birth to Don Juan Ornate colonizer of New Mexico.

The brother of Juan's father was a Corregidor in the town of Aguilar de Campoo in the province of Palencia in Castille-Leon Spain. A Corregidor presided over the elected Council to insure royal jurisdiction. He also collected taxes and acted as a judge.

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000001500799836

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
